hypothesis: In ensemble-consensus semi-supervised learning for molecular graphs, replacing a homogeneous ensemble with a parameter-matched heterogeneous ensemble (e.g., equal mix of GCN, GIN, and GatedGCN) will yield statistically significant improvements in both single-member test performance (lower MAE on QM9, higher AUROC on ogbg-molhiv) and calibration (lower ECE) because the more diverse consensus provides higher-quality targets.
